blocks.body: Past Perfect: Questions and Short Answers — Had you...?, Had she...? and short answers with had / hadn't.

{"level":"A2–B1","intro":"In Past Perfect questions, we put had before the subject: Had + subject + V3?","rules":[{"label":"Had + subject + V3?","color":"emerald","text":"This is the basic yes/no question pattern.","example":"Had you finished before 6 p.m.?"},{"label":"Wh-questions","color":"blue","text":"You can add what, why, where, when, or how long at the start.","example":"Why had she left so early?"},{"label":"Short answers","color":"amber","text":"Use had or hadn't in short answers.","example":"Yes, I had. / No, they hadn't."}]}

{"title":"1. Basic question patterns","intro":"Remember the word order in Past Perfect questions.","items":[{"label":"Yes / No","title":"Had + subject + V3?","subtitle":"Had she arrived before noon?"},{"label":"Wh-question","title":"Wh-word + had + subject + V3?","subtitle":"Why had they left before the end?"},{"label":"Short yes","title":"Yes, ... had.","subtitle":"Yes, I had. / Yes, he had."},{"label":"Short no","title":"No, ... hadn't.","subtitle":"No, we hadn't. / No, she hadn't."}]}

{"title":"2. When we ask these questions","sections":[{"label":"Earlier action","color":"emerald","description":"Ask whether the action happened before another event in the past.","examples":[{"en":"Had they left before the rain started?","ua":"Did their leaving happen first?"},{"en":"Had you met him before that day?","ua":"Was there an earlier meeting?"}]},{"label":"Before a past moment","color":"blue","description":"Ask whether the result was already ready at that moment.","examples":[{"en":"Had she finished the report by Friday?","ua":"Was the report complete by that point?"},{"en":"Had you packed everything by 8 a.m.?","ua":"Was everything ready by then?"}]},{"label":"Reason or explanation","color":"amber","description":"Wh-questions help you find the reason for the earlier action.","examples":[{"en":"Why had he changed his mind before the meeting?","ua":"The question asks about the earlier reason."},{"en":"Why had they never visited that place before?","ua":"The speaker wants the background explanation."}]},{"label":"Short answers","color":"violet","description":"Answer briefly when a full sentence is not necessary.","examples":[{"en":"Had you seen the film before? Yes, I had.","ua":"A short answer is enough here."},{"en":"Had she called before lunch? No, she hadn't.","ua":"Use had or hadn't in the reply."}]}]}

{"title":"3. Word order","intro":"See where had stands in different question types.","rows":[{"en":"Had you finished before the teacher arrived?","ua":"had + you + finished","note":"yes/no question"},{"en":"Had she ever seen snow before that trip?","ua":"had + she + ever seen","note":"question with ever"},{"en":"Why had they left so early?","ua":"why + had + they + left","note":"wh-question"},{"en":"Had we met before? Yes, we had.","ua":"had ... ? / yes, ... had","note":"short answer"}],"warning":"Do not say Did you had finished? In Past Perfect, the auxiliary is already had."}

{"title":"4. Short summary","items":["Past Perfect questions = Had + subject + V3?","Wh-questions follow the pattern Wh-word + had + subject + V3?","Use had or hadn't in short answers.","Do not add did, because this tense already has its auxiliary."]}
